seo.title: Building Code Generation with Flowise + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Flowise + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Flowise enables rapid development of code generation agents through its drag-and-drop interface, combining LLM chains, vector stores, and custom tools for iterative code refinement. However, unvalidated outputs, silent tool failures, and context loss in multi-agent flows can produce unsafe or incorrect code. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure code safety, syntax validity, and secure execution before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Flowise
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Flowise code generation flow starts with a user prompt routed to an LLM agent node that generates initial code. This feeds into a sandbox execution tool node for runtime testing, followed by a validation agent that checks syntax, safety, and test results. If issues are detected, the flow loops back through a refinement agent using vector store retrieval of similar code examples. Custom tool nodes handle dependency installation and credential scanning, with chatflow callbacks capturing intermediate outputs for monitoring.

Vector stores provide RAG context from code repositories, while multi-agent handoffs coordinate between generation, execution, and review phases. The high-risk nature demands verification at each stage to prevent malicious code injection or dependency vulnerabilities.

risks.name: Unvalidated code injection
risks.description: LLM generates code with malicious payloads or credential leaks due to prompt contamination
risks.mitigation: Vex code safety scanner blocks execution of dangerous patterns before sandbox

risks.name: Silent sandbox failures
risks.description: Custom tool nodes fail without error propagation, returning invalid code to user
risks.mitigation: Vex callback handler captures and validates all tool outputs with structured checks

risks.name: Context loss in refinement loops
risks.description: Multi-agent handoffs lose execution feedback, causing infinite invalid iterations
risks.mitigation: Vex session context preservation ensures full trace across agent boundaries

risks.name: Unsafe dependency installation
risks.description: Generated code installs malicious npm packages via dynamic import statements
risks.mitigation: Vex dependency analyzer whitelists approved packages before execution
